steps.title: Find the exponent change
steps.content:
The exponent in the model is
$$
\frac{4}{6}t=\frac{2}{3}t
$$
Over $\frac{3}{2}$ years, the exponent changes by
$$
\frac{2}{3}\cdot\frac{3}{2}=1
$$
So every $18$ months, the population is multiplied by $1.05$.

steps.title: Convert to percent increase
steps.content: A multiplier of $1.05$ means $$ (1.05-1)\cdot 100\%=5\% $$ The answer is C.
